title: nymphaea alba subsp. candida
intro: Nymphaea alba subsp. candida, also known as the white water lily, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nymphaea genus. It is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Nymphaea alba subsp. candida include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
